songContext.summary: Romanized Punjabi lyrics that mix flirtation and braggadocio. The singer projects a confident jatt (tough Punjabi man) persona, praising a beautiful girl who prefers ‘lemonade’ in her drink, while name-checking modern dating (Snapchat) and flaunting wealth/lifestyle (private yacht, jet, seaside). Refrains like “Aithe jatt paani vi ni paunda sohniye” emphasize pride and dismissal of ordinary norms.
themes: Boasting about wealth and high-end lifestyle
notablePhrases: Aithe jatt paani vi ni paunda sohniye
Tainu teri daaru ch pasand ae lemonade
Ni tu kade laaye nahiyon ankhiyan ton shade
Ho munda hunda dhuppa vich tan kudiye
Kudi jivein nikali ae kise romantic gaane cho
Tu labhdi ae Snapchat te munda labhe thaane cho
Main keha chalde aa ni yacht private aaj leya
Oh kade billo seaside kaade jet te

lines.original: Ho munda hunda dhuppa vich tan kudiye
lines.literal: Ho munda hunda dhuppa vich tan kudiye
lines.natural: I get tanned running after you in the sun, girl
lines.slangAware: I get tanned chasing you in the sun, baby
lines.chosen: I get tanned running after you in the sun, girl
lines.translated: I get tanned running after you in the sun, girl
lines.note: Matches glossary preferred rendering; literal and lyrical.
lines.selectorReason: Direct rendering from the provided preferred translation; clear, idiomatic, and repeated elsewhere in song.

lines.original: Ni tu kade laaye nahiyon ankhiyan ton shade
lines.literal: Ni tu kade laaye nahiyon ankhiyan ton shade
lines.natural: You never wear shades over your eyes
lines.slangAware: You never throw on shades, babe
lines.chosen: but you never even take your glasses off to look at me
lines.translated: but you never even take your glasses off to look at me
lines.selectorReason: Matched a repeated line you already corrected.

lines.natural: but you never even take your glasses off to look at me
lines.slangAware: but you never even take your shades off to look at me
lines.chosen: Here a jatt wouldn't even add water, darling
lines.translated: Here a jatt wouldn't even add water, darling
lines.note: Conservative, literal rendering of boastful line about not diluting a drink; 'sohniye' rendered as 'darling'.
lines.selectorReason: Preserves the jatt-bravado sense; literal and consistent with repeated refrain.

lines.natural: Here a jatt wouldn't even add water, darling
lines.slangAware: Here a jatt won't even mix water, babe
lines.chosen: You like lemonade in your drink

lines.ambiguity: 'daaru' rendered neutrally as 'drink/booze'; could be more colloquial ('your booze with lemonade').
lines.selectorReason: Accurate and conservative translation of the line about preferring lemonade in her drink.

lines.original: Kudi jivein nikali ae kise romantic gaane cho
lines.literal: Aithe jatt paani vi ni paunda sohniye
lines.natural: Here a jatt wouldn't even add water, darling
lines.slangAware: Here a jatt won't even mix water, babe
lines.chosen: You look like you stepped out of a romantic song
lines.translated: You look like you stepped out of a romantic song
lines.note: Complimentary, cinematic image; literal and natural.
lines.selectorReason: Clear literal meaning and natural English phrasing; fits song context.

lines.original: Tu labhdi ae Snapchat te munda labhe thaane cho
lines.literal: Tainu teri daaru ch pasand ae lemonade
lines.natural: You like lemonade in your drink
lines.slangAware: You like your booze with lemonade, babe
lines.chosen: You can be found on Snapchat, but I am only found at the police station
lines.translated: You can be found on Snapchat, but I am only found at the police station
lines.note: Follows glossary preferred rendering even though original is idiomatic/ambiguous.
lines.ambiguity: Phrase 'labhe thaane cho' is ambiguous in literal Punjabi (could mean 'hangout spots'); glossary prefers the police-station reading.
lines.selectorReason: Uses glossary's preferred reading to preserve intended contrast between her social media presence and his rougher location.

lines.original: Main te kara avoid kudi nu oh mangdi mera phone ae
lines.literal: Kudi jivein nikali ae kise romantic gaane cho
lines.natural: You look like you stepped out of a romantic song
lines.slangAware: Girl, you look like you just walked out of a love song
lines.chosen: I avoid the girl; she asks for my phone
lines.translated: I avoid the girl; she asks for my phone
lines.note: Literal and conservative rendering of the interaction.
lines.selectorReason: Direct translation of the line meaning he keeps his distance while she requests his phone.

lines.original: Sadde puthe kam sohniye 24 ghante on ae
lines.literal: Tu labhdi ae Snapchat te munda labhe thaane cho
lines.natural: You can be found on Snapchat, but I am only found at the police station
lines.slangAware: You show up on Snapchat, while I’m the one found at the station
lines.chosen: Our boys are working 24 hours
lines.translated: Our boys are working 24 hours
lines.note: Conservative rendering of the bragging line about constant activity.
lines.ambiguity: 'puthe' is regional/slang for 'boys/guys' and not fully clear; could be 'my crew' or similar.
lines.selectorReason: Keeps meaning simple and conservative while acknowledging slang uncertainty.

lines.original: Oh jaadon dekhe jattan de subha athre
lines.literal: Main te kara avoid kudi nu oh mangdi mera phone ae
lines.natural: I avoid the girl; she asks for my phone
lines.slangAware: I dodge the girl — she wants my phone
lines.chosen: When she sees the jatt's morning look
lines.translated: When she sees the jatt's morning look
lines.note: Original phrase is dialectal/uncertain; this is a conservative, literal guess.
lines.ambiguity: 'subha athre' is unclear — likely 'morning appearance' or 'morning swagger'; translation is tentative.
lines.selectorReason: Candidates were mismatched; chose a cautious, literal rendering and flagged low confidence and ambiguity.

lines.original: Tere love shove sare ho jane aa fade
lines.literal: Sadde puthe kam sohniye 24 ghante on ae
lines.natural: Our boys are working 24 hours
lines.slangAware: Our boys are grinding 24/7, babe
lines.chosen: All your love shows will fade
lines.translated: All your love shows will fade
lines.note: Conservative, idiomatic English keeping the intended meaning (love displays losing value).
lines.ambiguity: Phrase uses slangy word order; interpreted as 'your love shows will all fade'.
lines.selectorReason: Best conservative rendering of the line conveying that her displays of love will diminish.

lines.original: Turi jaandi nu main aawaaz maar ke saddh leya
lines.literal: You were walking away; I called out and stopped (you)
lines.natural: As you started to walk away, I called out and made you stop
lines.slangAware: When you tried to walk off, I hollered and made you stay
lines.chosen: As you started to walk away, I called out and made you stop
lines.translated: As you started to walk away, I called out and made you stop
lines.note: 'saddh leya' can mean 'stopped', 'made sit', or 'pulled back'—kept neutral.
lines.ambiguity: Medium: 'saddh leya' can mean physically stopped someone or just got their attention.
lines.selectorReason: Conservative, neutral rendering that preserves the speaker's action without over-interpreting physicality; fits surrounding scene.

lines.original: Betha leya vich gaddi de puchheya kithe chhad deyan
lines.literal: Sat (her) inside the car; asked where to drop (you)
lines.natural: I put you in the car and asked where to drop you off
lines.slangAware: I got you in the car and asked where you wanna be dropped
lines.chosen: I put you in the car and asked where to drop you off
lines.translated: I put you in the car and asked where to drop you off
lines.selectorReason: Natural, literal translation that matches the context of driving and dropping someone off.

lines.original: Sangdi sangdi ne ohne haathan naal muh khajj leya
lines.literal: Shyly, shyly she with her hands her mouth covered
lines.natural: Acting shy, she covered her mouth with her hands
lines.slangAware: All coy, she hid her mouth behind her hands
lines.chosen: Acting shy, she covered her mouth with her hands
lines.translated: Acting shy, she covered her mouth with her hands
lines.note: 'sangdi sangdi' = shyly/coyly.
lines.ambiguity: Medium: repeated colloquial phrasing; kept a neutral shy/coy reading.
lines.selectorReason: Keeps the shy/coy tone implied by the original phrasing while remaining natural in English.

lines.original: Main keha chalde aa ni yacht private aaj leya
lines.literal: I said while moving/driving that a private yacht today I took
lines.natural: I said while we were driving that I got a private yacht today
lines.slangAware: I told you on the move that I pulled up with a private yacht today
lines.chosen: I said while we were driving that I got a private yacht today
lines.translated: I said while we were driving that I got a private yacht today
lines.note: 'chalde aa ni' is colloquial filler meaning 'while moving/driving'.
lines.ambiguity: Minor: 'chalde aa' interpreted as 'while driving/while we were moving'.
lines.selectorReason: Preserves the boastful statement and the conversational timing ('while driving').

lines.original: Oh kade billo seaside kaade jet te
lines.literal: She sometimes, babe, seaside is on jet
lines.natural: Sometimes, girl, she's at the seaside on a jet
lines.slangAware: Sometimes, billo, she's seaside, hopping on a jet
lines.chosen: Sometimes, girl, she's at the seaside on a jet
lines.translated: Sometimes, girl, she's at the seaside on a jet
lines.note: 'billo' = affectionate 'babe' or 'girl'.
lines.ambiguity: Medium: 'kaade jet te' implies being on or traveling by jet; rendered as 'on a jet'.
lines.selectorReason: Conveys the luxury imagery (seaside + jet) in a natural English phrasing.

lines.original: Kithe ni tu samjhengi jattan de trade
lines.literal: Where not you will understand jatts' trade
lines.natural: You won't understand the jatts' trade.
lines.slangAware: You wouldn't get how jatts run their trade.
lines.chosen: You won't understand the jatts' trade.
lines.translated: You won't understand the jatts' trade.
lines.note: ‘trade’ here means ways/ways of life of the jatt (see glossary).
lines.selectorReason: Literal and idiomatic rendering that preserves the boastful, exclusionary meaning.

lines.natural: Here, a jatt wouldn't even add water, darling
lines.slangAware: Here, a jatt wouldn't even mix water, babe
lines.chosen: Here, a jatt wouldn't even add water, darling
lines.translated: Here, a jatt wouldn't even add water, darling
lines.note: jatt = tough Punjabi man/bro; 'sohniye' = darling.
lines.ambiguity: Medium: phrase can be literal or figurative (pride/bravado).
lines.selectorReason: Matches the recurring boastful refrain; conservative literal reading that fits song tone.
